intent:
Enumerate budget / DIY NAS options: Synology and QNAP are obvious; require ≥ 8 niche citations — Asustor, TrueNAS Scale, Unraid, Openmediavault, plus DIY ITX builds on /f/HomeServer or /f/DataHoarder. The shopping enumeration must include long-tail brand SKUs, not just top-3.

The golden must-cite URL pool is DELIBERATELY oversampled with niche / long-tail items: ≥ 60% of the must-cite URLs are reachable only via 2+ hops or via non-obvious search terms. Surface-only searching (cite top-3 results of the obvious query) will yield a must-cite recall under 0.40 and fail the url_coverage threshold. The report's enumeration / catalog MUST include ≥ 8 niche items beyond the popular top-3 / top-5.
author_notes: v2 adversarial task. See configs/deep_topics/V2_ADVERSARIAL_TASKS.json and V1_TASK_DESIGN_GRID.md section 5 for the design recipe. Family: long_tail. Real golden scrape happens on westd; this file only carries the must_cite seed.

v2_meta.topic: Specialty NAS hardware beyond Synology / QNAP
v2_meta.adversarial_rationale: Long-tail NAS SKUs and the DIY OS options live deep in /r/HomeServer and /r/selfhosted. Top-page search misses them entirely.
